note: Seeded preview. The 3 detailed mockups (ChatGPT/Claude/Gemini) and branded placeholders render before the first automated run; the weekly workflow replaces them with real full-page PNG captures.

captures.analysis: ChatGPT leads with a near-blank white canvas and a single centered prompt — 'What can I help with?' — deferring all chrome to a muted left rail. Hierarchy is radically reductive: one composer, a couple of suggestion chips, and the model switcher tucked at top. The green send affordance is the only saturated element, pulling the eye straight to the primary action.

Claude opts for a warm, paper-like cream ground rather than clinical white, giving the surface an editorial, softer feel. A time-aware greeting and a raised composer card create a calm focal point, while intent chips (Write / Learn / Code) frame the tool as task-oriented. The terracotta accent is used sparingly on the logo, primary button, and send control to keep a single consistent brand thread.

Gemini anchors the page with an oversized gradient greeting that doubles as brand signature, spanning Google's blue-to-magenta spectrum. The layout is left-aligned and roomier than its peers, pairing the hero with a row of suggestion cards that preview capabilities rather than starting blank. The rounded 'Ask Gemini' pill and pill-shaped new-chat button echo Material 3's fully-rounded geometry throughout.

Perplexity foregrounds answers over chat — the composer sits centre-stage with 'Search / Research / Labs' mode pills, framing it as an answer engine rather than a chatbot. A teal accent and generous whitespace keep it calm, while source citations are treated as first-class UI, reinforcing its research positioning.

Microsoft Copilot leans into a friendly, consumer feel — a soft gradient backdrop, a large centred greeting, and a rounded composer with voice and image entry points. It reads warmer and more playful than its enterprise sibling in Microsoft 365, prioritising approachability over density.

Le Chat keeps a crisp, European-minimal aesthetic with Mistral's orange accent used sparingly against near-white. The composer is front and centre with quick tool toggles (web, code, image), and the overall hierarchy is flat and fast, matching the brand's efficiency-first positioning.

Grok commits to a stark monochrome, near-black canvas that signals its X / edgy positioning. The layout is reductive — a centred composer with model and mode toggles — and typography does the heavy lifting, with almost no chrome or colour to distract from the input.

DeepSeek mirrors the reductive white-canvas pattern ChatGPT popularised, with a single centred composer. Its signature move is the pair of toggles beneath the input — 'DeepThink (R1)' for visible reasoning and 'Search' — surfacing its reasoning model as a first-class, one-tap choice.

Meta AI is image- and generation-forward, with a bold blue-to-purple identity and prominent 'Imagine' image prompts rather than a blank chat. The layout borrows from Meta's social surfaces — rounded, media-rich cards — and assumes a casual, consumer audience woven into Instagram and WhatsApp.

Manus presents as an autonomous agent, not a chatbot — the interface is a split view pairing a conversation with a live 'computer' workspace where tasks execute step by step. The aesthetic is dark and professional, and the primary affordance is delegating a whole task rather than exchanging messages.

Poe is an aggregator: a left rail lists dozens of bots (GPT, Claude, and community creations) and the home is a directory grid rather than a single assistant. A purple accent and card-based browsing frame it as an app store for models, optimising for discovery and switching.

Character.AI is entertainment-first — the home is a grid of avatar cards for user-created characters, with playful imagery and a discovery feed. Chat is secondary to browsing personalities, and the visual language (rounded avatars, vibrant cards) signals roleplay and companionship over productivity.

HuggingChat wears its open-source ethos plainly — a lightweight, developer-leaning layout with a prominent open-model picker and Hugging Face's yellow-orange accent. It is deliberately unpolished-by-design, prioritising transparency (which model, which tools) over consumer gloss.

Qwen (Alibaba) is feature-dense, exposing model variants and multimodal tabs (chat, image, video) up front. A violet accent and a busier toolbar reflect a do-everything positioning, packing more capability into the first screen than its Western minimalist peers.

Kimi (Moonshot AI) centres long-context and document work — file and URL upload are prominent, and the composer invites large inputs rather than short prompts. A clean, slightly cool palette with a violet accent keeps focus on the reading and analysis workflow it is known for.

You.com is a search-chat hybrid — a mode switcher lets users pivot between web search and AI answers, foregrounding productivity and sourcing. The layout stays utilitarian and information-dense, positioning it closer to a smart search engine than a personality-driven assistant.

Pi (Inflection) is the outlier on tone — a warm cream palette, soft typography, and a voice-forward, one-message-at-a-time cadence designed to feel like a supportive conversation. It deliberately strips away tools and density to emphasise emotional, unhurried dialogue.
